messages.author: feurgroup
messages.content:
# FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions
~~───────────────────────~~
## Q: What is Create Submarine?
**A:** Create Submarine aims to provide a realistic underwater exploration experience built on top of Create Aeronautics.
The addon features a pressure system, implosion mechanics, deep-sea exploration, custom biomes, creatures and much more.
Directly inspired by Subnautica and Iron Lung.

~~───────────────────────~~
## Q: I want to become a Tester — how do I apply?
**A:** It is fairly simple. Whenever spots are available, you can apply in the <#1499034566780915794> channel.
In return, your mission is to report every bug you encounter.

~~───────────────────────~~

messages.author: feurgroup
messages.content:
## Q: With the upcoming Create Aeronautics update introducing boats, will the addon be adapted?
**A:** Yes, this has been planned from an architectural standpoint.
Once the update is released, we will drop our current system and hook directly into CA's implementation.
This will allow for much greater stability overall.

~~───────────────────────~~
## Q: My sub is constantly imploding, what can I do
**A:** You can either make your sub more resistant by using resistant blocks for your hull (like iron blocks)  or you can also disable implosions in the mod’s configuration.

~~───────────────────────~~
## Q: How can I access the Abyss ?
**A:** For now you can’t access the dimension in survival, it is still in development and has nothing to offer. However if you truly want to access it you can enable it in the mod’s configuration and then type `/execute in create_submarine:abyss run tp @s ~ ~ ~`

~~───────────────────────~~
## Q: I want to contribute to the mod’s development, how can I ?
**A:** What we are looking for are:
- coders
- artists
- designers (concepts drawings)
- builders (Abyss structures)
- terrain generation pros (Overworld and Abyss generation)
- translators
If you want to become any of these, you can start showcasing your works in <#1500875117842923623> and ask <@1488807506720587806> for specific things to do in your field.
You can find the GitHub [right here](https://github.com/Maxenonyme/Create-Deep-Seas)

messages.author: __noxaeterna__
messages.content:
Feedback report:
- I prefer other FE suppliers to crafts & additions, and there's nothing in the modpage stating it's a dependency, yet the mod crashes without it. I believe it's currently a requirement purely for a ponder stating a block needs FE, showing wires from crafts & additions. as a modpack dev I'd love this dependency to be optional
- rather than disabling implosion entirely, I think it would be ideal to have a data-driven whitelist/blacklist option for implosion blocks. As a blacklist, implosion would be calculated only with the blocks marked in the blacklist, as "fragile"
- adding some rotation to the gui items would make them more aesthetic in the inventory. The same rotation as default blocks would look best imo

messages.author: techtastic
messages.content:
Let me clarity since I got the devs eyes

I use Minotaur (Modrinth's official Gradle plugin) and CurseGradle (CurseForge's official Gradle plugin) for uploading JARs

I use a manually ran GitHub Actions YAML file to build and upload the mod to the specific platforms via these plugins' Gradle tasks

I also made it use environment variables so I dont share API keys accidentally (because of course)

messages.author: techtastic
messages.content:

- [release.yml](<https://github.com/TechTastic/CC-Deep-Seas/blob/master/.github%2Fworkflows%2Frelease.yml>)

messages.author: maxenonyme
messages.content:
# Planned Features
~~──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────~~
Everything that is planned and confirmed for Create Submarine. Not everything is written down yet; these are ideas from YOU! And thank you.

## Mechanics
- A river and ocean connection system allowing seamless biome transitions between bodies of water

## Pressure
- A dedicated reinforced glass block with higher depth resistance than standard glass
- A visible in-game pressure gauge displayed on the HUD
- An optimised pressure calculation based on both the Y coordinate and the actual water column above the submarine

## Water Management
- Numerical air and water counters visible to the player
- Block-by-block water flooding inside the submarine in the event of a breach
- Proper water drainage handling when a block is manually broken while submerged

## Aesthetics
- Cracking glass texture that visually evolves as pressure increases
- Progressive darkening of the environment as the submarine descends deeper
- Increased oxygen drain rate based on current depth

## Damage
- A compression and implosion system that physically pushes blocks inward toward the center upon structural failure
- A structural breach detection system checking whether a damaged block connects the exterior and interior of the hull
- Breach severity proportional to the size of the submarine rather than a fixed threshold
- Progressive flooding once the first breach opens, adding weight and causing the vessel to sink

## Progression
- A tier system for hull materials with distinct resistance values and a clear upgrade hierarchy

## World
- Custom underwater biomes including deep-sea and abyss zones
- New hostile entities including Leviathan-class creatures

~~──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────~~
This list is subject to change as development progresses.

messages.author: maxenonyme
messages.content:
# Retained Community Ideas
~~──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────~~
Ideas submitted by the community that have been approved and added to the project plans.

~~──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────~~

✅  **Material Tier Progression System** — suggested by <@452710863841787904>
> Hull materials follow a progression from weakest to most resistant :
> **Andesite → Brass → Steel → Titanium → Abyss modded ores**
> Each tier reduces hull vulnerability to breaches (0.5 / 0.3 / 0.2 / 0.1 / 0).
> To unlock deeper tiers, the player must exit the submarine and collect materials in increasingly dangerous biomes — deliberately inducing thalassophobia.
> 
> **Reinforced Plating interaction** : using reinforced plating lowers the number of breaches required to sink the vessel, but early-tier materials add a slight vulnerability that partially counteracts this bonus.
>
> **World depth extension** : world generation extended down to Y=-156 or Y=-256 for realistic deep-sea navigation.

~~──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────~~

messages.author: maxenonyme
messages.content: First, don't learn Minecraft modding directly, just Java, the basics, like creating a function, a condition, etc. It's quite simple. Then you have two choices: either you go directly to Kaupen's tutorials and create your first items, or you study the theory, that is, what a mixin is, what the mod's BUS is, where you save items, etc. This avoids going in blind.

messages.author: maxenonyme
messages.content: And to level up and do increasingly crazy things, all you have to do is something very simple: look at the code of other mods. For example, in my case, I looked at how the Simulated team made their rope, then I copied it and added some features, and that's how I got the steel cable.

messages.author: maxenonyme
messages.content: There's also something that helps, which is developing something else, like me, before doing modding, developing games on UE5

messages.author: offical_cogfly_06638
messages.content: you also somewhat need to be able to break a problem into much easier steps

messages.author: o51445
messages.content:
A small summary of the Abyss biome: overall, it conveys the atmosphere really well, but the darkness can sometimes get in the way. The ecosystem is pretty solid, although more variety wouldn’t hurt, since there are a bit too few fish.
I’d especially like to mention the Deep Coral biome — it’s incredibly beautiful. The leviathans are also a masterpiece on their own.
Of course, I’d like there to be more interesting structures, but even as it is, the biome was both interesting to explore and genuinely unsettling to be in.
